item.title: LSDN: A Lightweight Dual-Domain Attention Network with Adaptive Anchors for Ship Detection in Remote Sensing Scenarios
item.dc:creator: Shounan Wang
Chen Jia
Hui Liu
Yifan Yin
Xu Cheng
Shengyong Chen
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Detecting small target ships (with pixel dimensions below 32×32) in remote sensing imagery is crucial for maintaining maritime traffic safety. However, achieving high accuracy with low computational cost remains challenging due to the dense scale distribution and the weak visual cues of small ship targets. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Lightweight Ship Detection Network (LSDN), which is capable of accurately localizing small ships in multi-scale scenarios within remote sensing images, while maintaining a low parameter count and computational cost. Specifically, LSDN integrates three core components: First, the Adaptive Feature Enhancement Module (AFEM) employs a multi-level feature fusion strategy to enhance shallow feature representation, effectively preserving relevant information for small targets. Second, a lightweight backbone network MESA-ShipDetection incorporates a Multi-scale Efficient Strengthened Attention (MESA) to dynamically modulate feature importance across spatial and scale domains. Third, a dynamic anchor generation strategy constructs three sets of learnable anchor parameters based on target size distributions, thereby optimizing bounding box matching and localization accuracy. Experiments on the Airbus-ship dataset demonstrate that LSDN achieves a detection accuracy of mAP at 81.60% with only 3.65M parameters, representing a 9.75% improvement over existing SOTA methods. Additionally, ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each module, with the AFEM increasing the recall rate of small targets by 7.83%, and the dynamic anchor strategy improving localization accuracy by 5.49%. This study offers a practical and efficient solution for real-time ship detection in resource-constrained scenarios. Published: 2026-03-23T20:07:27+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.843 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shounan Wang; Chen Jia; Hui Liu; Yifan Yin; Xu Cheng; Shengyong Chen

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3676813

Relevance: 0.843 (must_read)

Detecting small target ships (with pixel dimensions below 32×32) in remote sensing imagery is crucial for maintaining maritime traffic safety. However, achieving high accuracy with low computational cost remains challenging due to the dense scale distribution and the weak visual cues of small ship targets. To address these limitations, we propose a novel Lightweight Ship Detection Network (LSDN), which is capable of accurately localizing small ships in multi-scale scenarios within remote sensing images, while maintaining a low parameter count and computational cost. Specifically, LSDN integrates three core components: First, the Adaptive Feature Enhancement Module (AFEM) employs a multi-level feature fusion strategy to enhance shallow feature representation, effectively preserving relevant information for small targets. Second, a lightweight backbone network MESA-ShipDetection incorporates a Multi-scale Efficient Strengthened Attention (MESA) to dynamically modulate feature importance across spatial and scale domains. Third, a dynamic anchor generation strategy constructs three sets of learnable anchor parameters based on target size distributions, thereby optimizing bounding box matching and localization accuracy. Experiments on the Airbus-ship dataset demonstrate that LSDN achieves a detection accuracy of mAP at 81.60% with only 3.65M parameters, representing a 9.75% improvement over existing SOTA methods. Additionally, ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each module, with the AFEM increasing the recall rate of small targets by 7.83%, and the dynamic anchor strategy improving localization accuracy by 5.49%. This study offers a practical and efficient solution for real-time ship detection in resource-constrained scenarios.

item.title: FEDA-Net: A Feature Enhancement and Dynamic Alignment Network for SAR Ship Detection
item.dc:creator: Shuaiqi Liu
Yue Yu
Ruiyi Feng
Bing Li
Yudong Zhang
Qi Hu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Due to the imaging mechanism of synthetic aperture radar (SAR), speckle noise often obscures details of the ship and leads to the loss of semantic information. Meanwhile, the presence of targets at extremely diverse scales challenges the capacity of single-feature representation. In addition, geometric deformations of ships tend to induce localization inaccuracies and reduce classification confidence. These issues collectively aggravate the competition and imbalance between classification and localization tasks. In response to these challenges, we propose a feature enhancement and dynamic alignment network (FEDA-Net) to improve ship detection in SAR images. First, a lightweight co-attention network (LCA-Net) is designed, which synergistically captures both local and global multi-scale features through a multi-level convolutional architecture and a dual-dimensional attention mechanism, thereby enhancing the extraction of noise-robust multi-scale features. Second, a cross-stage fusion pyramid network (CSFPN) is constructed to achieve complementary integration of semantic and detailed information via bidirectional feature fusion. During training, the fusion module adopts a multi-branch topology to enrich multi-scale feature information, while during inference, it is converted into a single-branch structure through structural re-parameterization, effectively reducing parameter redundancy. Finally, we innovatively propose a dynamic alignment detection head (DADH), which adopts parameter sharing and adaptive task-decoupling strategies to separate the classification and localization branches, while incorporating a bidirectional interaction mechanism to effectively balance the competition between the two tasks. On the SSDD and HRSID datasets, the proposed method achieves accuracies of 99.1% and 93.2%, respectively, while achieving a reduction in model parameters. Furthermore, the model demonstrates superior generalization performance on the large-scale LS-SSDD-v1.0 dataset. Published: 2026-03-23T20:08:23+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.837 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Shuaiqi Liu; Yue Yu; Ruiyi Feng; Bing Li; Yudong Zhang; Qi Hu

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3676630

Relevance: 0.837 (must_read)

Due to the imaging mechanism of synthetic aperture radar (SAR), speckle noise often obscures details of the ship and leads to the loss of semantic information. Meanwhile, the presence of targets at extremely diverse scales challenges the capacity of single-feature representation. In addition, geometric deformations of ships tend to induce localization inaccuracies and reduce classification confidence. These issues collectively aggravate the competition and imbalance between classification and localization tasks. In response to these challenges, we propose a feature enhancement and dynamic alignment network (FEDA-Net) to improve ship detection in SAR images. First, a lightweight co-attention network (LCA-Net) is designed, which synergistically captures both local and global multi-scale features through a multi-level convolutional architecture and a dual-dimensional attention mechanism, thereby enhancing the extraction of noise-robust multi-scale features. Second, a cross-stage fusion pyramid network (CSFPN) is constructed to achieve complementary integration of semantic and detailed information via bidirectional feature fusion. During training, the fusion module adopts a multi-branch topology to enrich multi-scale feature information, while during inference, it is converted into a single-branch structure through structural re-parameterization, effectively reducing parameter redundancy. Finally, we innovatively propose a dynamic alignment detection head (DADH), which adopts parameter sharing and adaptive task-decoupling strategies to separate the classification and localization branches, while incorporating a bidirectional interaction mechanism to effectively balance the competition between the two tasks. On the SSDD and HRSID datasets, the proposed method achieves accuracies of 99.1% and 93.2%, respectively, while achieving a reduction in model parameters. Furthermore, the model demonstrates superior generalization performance on the large-scale LS-SSDD-v1.0 dataset.

item.title: DFAS-CMA: A Decoupled Feature Adaptive Sharing and Cross-Modulation Approach for UAV Multi-Source Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Xuehu Duan
Xiangqing Liu
Zihao Li
Jun Lei
Shuohao Li
Jun Zhang
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: The Infrared-Visible Image Object Detection (IVIOD) algorithm is used to solve the problem of degraded object detection accuracy in complex scenes with a single source by fusing the complementary properties of infrared and visible images. Currently, there are fewer IVIOD researches for unmanned aerial vehicles, and most methods cannot fully consider sensor modal differences and imbalances, causing failure to effectively improve detection accuracy when fusing heterogeneous data. To address these issues, we propose an end-to-end multi-source image object detection framework based on the Decoupled Feature Adaptive Sharing and Cross-Modulation Approach (DFAS-CMA), aiming to improve the accuracy of object detection in infrared and visible images from the viewpoint of unmanned aerial vehicle. DFAS-CMA deploys spatial-channel decoupled re-parametric network to extract decoupled features from different dimensional of multi-source images, effectively capturing small object information and optimizing the feature extraction process. An adaptive feature weight sharing mechanism is then proposed, which effectively mitigates sensor modal differences and imbalances by adaptively fusing heterogeneous data. Ultimately, to improve the robustness of DFAS-CMA in scenarios with small object multi-scale variations, a multi-scale feature cross-modulation aggregation strategy is introduced. Experimental results show that the proposed method improves the mAP 0.5 and mAP 0.5: 0.95 by 3.3% and 3.0% , respectively, on the VTUAV-Det dataset. On the DroneVehicle dataset, mAP 0.5 and mAP 0.5: 0.95 are improved by 7.1% and 7.0% , respectively, demonstrating the superiority of DFAS-CMA in enhancing the accuracy and generalization ability of multi-source image object detection. Published: 2026-03-24T00:21:26+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.828 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xuehu Duan; Xiangqing Liu; Zihao Li; Jun Lei; Shuohao Li; Jun Zhang

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104320

Relevance: 0.828 (must_read)

The Infrared-Visible Image Object Detection (IVIOD) algorithm is used to solve the problem of degraded object detection accuracy in complex scenes with a single source by fusing the complementary properties of infrared and visible images. Currently, there are fewer IVIOD researches for unmanned aerial vehicles, and most methods cannot fully consider sensor modal differences and imbalances, causing failure to effectively improve detection accuracy when fusing heterogeneous data. To address these issues, we propose an end-to-end multi-source image object detection framework based on the Decoupled Feature Adaptive Sharing and Cross-Modulation Approach (DFAS-CMA), aiming to improve the accuracy of object detection in infrared and visible images from the viewpoint of unmanned aerial vehicle. DFAS-CMA deploys spatial-channel decoupled re-parametric network to extract decoupled features from different dimensional of multi-source images, effectively capturing small object information and optimizing the feature extraction process. An adaptive feature weight sharing mechanism is then proposed, which effectively mitigates sensor modal differences and imbalances by adaptively fusing heterogeneous data. Ultimately, to improve the robustness of DFAS-CMA in scenarios with small object multi-scale variations, a multi-scale feature cross-modulation aggregation strategy is introduced. Experimental results show that the proposed method improves the mAP 0.5 and mAP 0.5: 0.95 by 3.3% and 3.0% , respectively, on the VTUAV-Det dataset. On the DroneVehicle dataset, mAP 0.5 and mAP 0.5: 0.95 are improved by 7.1% and 7.0% , respectively, demonstrating the superiority of DFAS-CMA in enhancing the accuracy and generalization ability of multi-source image object detection.

item.title: Rethinking SAR ATR: A Target-Aware Frequency-Spatial Enhancement Framework with Noise-Resilient Knowledge Guidance
item.dc:creator: Yansong Lin
Zihan Cheng
Jielei Wang
Guoming Lua
Zongyong Cui
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: Synthetic aperture radar automatic target recognition (SAR ATR) is of considerable importance in marine navigation and disaster monitoring. However, the coherent speckle noise inherent in SAR imagery often obscures salient target features, leading to degraded recognition accuracy and limited model generalization. To address this issue, this paper proposes a target-aware frequency-spatial enhancement framework with noise-resilient knowledge guidance (FSCE) for SAR target recognition. The proposed framework incorporates a frequency-spatial shallow feature adaptive enhancement (DSAF) module, which processes shallow features through spatial multi-scale convolution and frequency-domain wavelet convolution. In addition, a teacher-student learning paradigm combined with an online knowledge distillation method (KD) is employed to guide the student network to focus more effectively on target regions, thereby enhancing its robustness to high-noise backgrounds. Through the collaborative optimization of attention transfer and noise-resilient representation learning, the proposed approach significantly improves the stability of target recognition under noisy conditions. Based on the FSCE framework, two network architectures with different performance emphases are developed: lightweight DSAFNet-M and high-precision DSAFNet-L. Extensive experiments are conducted on the MSTAR, FUSARShip and OpenSARShip datasets. The results show that DSAFNet-L achieves competitive or superior performance compared with various methods on three datasets; DSAFNet-M significantly reduces the model complexity while maintaining comparable accuracy. These results indicate that the proposed FSCE framework exhibits strong cross-model generalization. Published: 2026-03-23T04:35:31+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.821 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yansong Lin; Zihan Cheng; Jielei Wang; Guoming Lua; Zongyong Cui

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.821 (must_read)

Synthetic aperture radar automatic target recognition (SAR ATR) is of considerable importance in marine navigation and disaster monitoring. However, the coherent speckle noise inherent in SAR imagery often obscures salient target features, leading to degraded recognition accuracy and limited model generalization. To address this issue, this paper proposes a target-aware frequency-spatial enhancement framework with noise-resilient knowledge guidance (FSCE) for SAR target recognition. The proposed framework incorporates a frequency-spatial shallow feature adaptive enhancement (DSAF) module, which processes shallow features through spatial multi-scale convolution and frequency-domain wavelet convolution. In addition, a teacher-student learning paradigm combined with an online knowledge distillation method (KD) is employed to guide the student network to focus more effectively on target regions, thereby enhancing its robustness to high-noise backgrounds. Through the collaborative optimization of attention transfer and noise-resilient representation learning, the proposed approach significantly improves the stability of target recognition under noisy conditions. Based on the FSCE framework, two network architectures with different performance emphases are developed: lightweight DSAFNet-M and high-precision DSAFNet-L. Extensive experiments are conducted on the MSTAR, FUSARShip and OpenSARShip datasets. The results show that DSAFNet-L achieves competitive or superior performance compared with various methods on three datasets; DSAFNet-M significantly reduces the model complexity while maintaining comparable accuracy. These results indicate that the proposed FSCE framework exhibits strong cross-model generalization.

item.title: HFP-SAM: Hierarchical Frequency Prompted SAM for Efficient Marine Animal Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Pingping Zhang
Tianyu Yan
Yuhao Wang
Yang Liu
Tongdan Tang
Yili Ma
Long Lv
Feng Tian
Weibing Sun
Huchuan Lu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Marine Animal Segmentation (MAS) aims at identifying and segmenting marine animals from complex marine environments. Most of previous deep learning-based MAS methods struggle with the long-distance modeling issue. Recently, Segment Anything Model (SAM) has gained popularity in general image segmentation. However, it lacks of perceiving fine-grained details and frequency information. To this end, we propose a novel learning framework, named Hierarchical Frequency Prompted SAM (HFP-SAM) for high-performance MAS. First, we design a Frequency Guided Adapter (FGA) to efficiently inject marine scene information into the frozen SAM backbone through frequency domain prior masks. Additionally, we introduce a Frequency-aware Point Selection (FPS) to generate highlighted regions through frequency analysis. These regions are combined with the coarse predictions of SAM to generate point prompts and integrate into SAM’s decoder for fine predictions. Finally, to obtain comprehensive segmentation masks, we introduce a Full-View Mamba (FVM) to efficiently extract spatial and channel contextual information with linear computational complexity. Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate the superior performance of our approach. We will make our code publicly available upon the acceptance. Published: 2026-03-24T19:50:48+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.814 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Pingping Zhang; Tianyu Yan; Yuhao Wang; Yang Liu; Tongdan Tang; Yili Ma; Long Lv; Feng Tian; Weibing Sun; Huchuan Lu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3674678

Relevance: 0.814 (must_read)

Marine Animal Segmentation (MAS) aims at identifying and segmenting marine animals from complex marine environments. Most of previous deep learning-based MAS methods struggle with the long-distance modeling issue. Recently, Segment Anything Model (SAM) has gained popularity in general image segmentation. However, it lacks of perceiving fine-grained details and frequency information. To this end, we propose a novel learning framework, named Hierarchical Frequency Prompted SAM (HFP-SAM) for high-performance MAS. First, we design a Frequency Guided Adapter (FGA) to efficiently inject marine scene information into the frozen SAM backbone through frequency domain prior masks. Additionally, we introduce a Frequency-aware Point Selection (FPS) to generate highlighted regions through frequency analysis. These regions are combined with the coarse predictions of SAM to generate point prompts and integrate into SAM’s decoder for fine predictions. Finally, to obtain comprehensive segmentation masks, we introduce a Full-View Mamba (FVM) to efficiently extract spatial and channel contextual information with linear computational complexity. Extensive experiments on four public datasets demonstrate the superior performance of our approach. We will make our code publicly available upon the acceptance.

item.title: Modality Equilibrium Matters: Minor-Modality-Aware Adaptive Alternating for Cross-Modal Memory Enhancement
item.dc:creator: Xiang Shi
Rui Zhang
Jiawei Liu
Yinpeng Liu
Zhu Liang
Qikai Cheng
Wei Lu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Multimodal fusion is susceptible to modality imbalance, where dominant modalities overshadow weak ones, easily leading to biased learning and suboptimal fusion, especially for incomplete modality conditions. To address this problem, we introduce an Equilibrium Deviation Metric (EDM) to quantify this imbalance and verify, in both theoretical and empirical terms, that the optimization order of modalities plays a critical role in approaching equilibrium. In particular, we demonstrate that an EDM-ranked weak-to-strong schedule achieves the tightest convergence bound among all possible ordering strategies. Leveraging these insights, we design an alternating strategy that dynamically prioritises under-optimised modalities, plus a modality-mapping layer for feature alignment and a memory module for information filtering and inheritance. Our framework is compatible with both conventional and MLLM-based backbones. It achieves new state-of-the-art (SOTA) on four benchmarks (e.g., +3.36% on CREMA-D, +3.51% on Kinetics-400), and remains robust under missing-modality conditions. These findings highlight the value of modality scheduling, offering a principled alternative to conventional joint training. Published: 2026-03-23T20:07:33+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.814 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiang Shi; Rui Zhang; Jiawei Liu; Yinpeng Liu; Zhu Liang; Qikai Cheng; Wei Lu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3676535

Relevance: 0.814 (must_read)

Multimodal fusion is susceptible to modality imbalance, where dominant modalities overshadow weak ones, easily leading to biased learning and suboptimal fusion, especially for incomplete modality conditions. To address this problem, we introduce an Equilibrium Deviation Metric (EDM) to quantify this imbalance and verify, in both theoretical and empirical terms, that the optimization order of modalities plays a critical role in approaching equilibrium. In particular, we demonstrate that an EDM-ranked weak-to-strong schedule achieves the tightest convergence bound among all possible ordering strategies. Leveraging these insights, we design an alternating strategy that dynamically prioritises under-optimised modalities, plus a modality-mapping layer for feature alignment and a memory module for information filtering and inheritance. Our framework is compatible with both conventional and MLLM-based backbones. It achieves new state-of-the-art (SOTA) on four benchmarks (e.g., +3.36% on CREMA-D, +3.51% on Kinetics-400), and remains robust under missing-modality conditions. These findings highlight the value of modality scheduling, offering a principled alternative to conventional joint training.

item.title: CDEHAT: Conditional Diffusion-Assisted Enhanced Hybrid Attention Transformer for remote sensing imagery super-resolution
item.dc:creator: Xiande Wu
Rui Liu
Wei Wu
Haiping Yang
Liao Yang
item.prism:publicationName: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
item.description: Deep learning-based methods with hybrid architectures have achieved remarkable progress in remote sensing image super-resolution (SR). However, the absence of HR references makes LR-HR mapping priors ineffective for guiding reconstruction during inference. At the same time, CNNs and Transformers each have inherent limitations with long-range dependencies and local details, bringing difficulty in cross-scene SR adaptation. To address these issues, we propose the Conditional Diffusion-Assisted Enhanced Hybrid Attention Transformer (CDEHAT) for remote sensing images super-resolution, which combines diffusion prior learning and a hybrid attention mechanism. Specifically, CDEHAT adopts a two-branch collaborative architecture CDEHAT-Diff and CDEHAT-SR. CDEHAT-Diff explores the conditional degradation-to-restoration process between LR and HR images, learning latent LR-HR mapping patterns through diffusion modeling, which then guides the subsequent reconstruction stage. The diffusion-derived priors empower CDEHAT-SR to discover intrinsic super-resolution manifolds, facilitating precise recovery of fine-grained structural and textural details from degraded observations. At the same time, two enhanced hybrid attention transformer modules are designed to integrate multi-scale features in the spectral–spatial domain for better high-frequency information reconstruction. Experimental results on both simulated and real datasets show that our method significantly improves the performance of SR tasks, with an average PSNR improvement of 0.20 dB and 0.51 dB, and performs well in reconstructing details and recovering structures. The code and dataset will be available at https://github.com/cerulean136/CDEHAT . Published: 2026-03-23T14:24:18+00:00 Venue: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing Score: 0.812 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiande Wu; Rui Liu; Wei Wu; Haiping Yang; Liao Yang

Journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1016/j.isprsjprs.2026.03.002

Relevance: 0.812 (must_read)

Deep learning-based methods with hybrid architectures have achieved remarkable progress in remote sensing image super-resolution (SR). However, the absence of HR references makes LR-HR mapping priors ineffective for guiding reconstruction during inference. At the same time, CNNs and Transformers each have inherent limitations with long-range dependencies and local details, bringing difficulty in cross-scene SR adaptation. To address these issues, we propose the Conditional Diffusion-Assisted Enhanced Hybrid Attention Transformer (CDEHAT) for remote sensing images super-resolution, which combines diffusion prior learning and a hybrid attention mechanism. Specifically, CDEHAT adopts a two-branch collaborative architecture CDEHAT-Diff and CDEHAT-SR. CDEHAT-Diff explores the conditional degradation-to-restoration process between LR and HR images, learning latent LR-HR mapping patterns through diffusion modeling, which then guides the subsequent reconstruction stage. The diffusion-derived priors empower CDEHAT-SR to discover intrinsic super-resolution manifolds, facilitating precise recovery of fine-grained structural and textural details from degraded observations. At the same time, two enhanced hybrid attention transformer modules are designed to integrate multi-scale features in the spectral–spatial domain for better high-frequency information reconstruction. Experimental results on both simulated and real datasets show that our method significantly improves the performance of SR tasks, with an average PSNR improvement of 0.20 dB and 0.51 dB, and performs well in reconstructing details and recovering structures. The code and dataset will be available at https://github.com/cerulean136/CDEHAT .

item.title: Single-Domain Generalization: A Scattering Characteristic-Driven Cross-Attention Model for Oriented SAR Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Hailiang Huang
Huangxing Lin
Jingchao Guo
Yue Huang
Xinghao Ding
Huanqiang Zeng
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Oriented object detection in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery often struggles to generalize to unknown domains, due to severe distribution shifts. Single-domain generalization (single-DG) aims to tackle this challenge by learning robust representations from only one source domain. However, existing single-DG methods typically fail to capture the essential features of rotating objects, as they neglect the unique physical properties introduced by the SAR imaging process. To address this, this paper proposes the scattering characteristic-driven cross-attention model (SCCAM), which introduces a heuristic scattering representation to approximate the potential physical structures of SAR objects. Specifically, a pair of homologous images is constructed by perturbing the extremely low- and high-frequency components of the input SAR image, ensuring that they share domain-invariant information. The heuristic attribute scattering center (ASC) features are then extracted to guide a bidirectional cross-attention module, enabling progressive interactions between homologous features. This design effectively mitigates feature distribution discrepancies, thereby facilitating the learning of domain-invariant representations. Experimental results on six single-DG benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed SCCAM significantly outperforms multiple classic and state-of-the-art DG models in oriented object detection across unknown SAR domains. Published: 2026-03-24T19:48:43+00:00 Venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing Score: 0.810 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Hailiang Huang; Huangxing Lin; Jingchao Guo; Yue Huang; Xinghao Ding; Huanqiang Zeng

Journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/jstars.2026.3677034

Relevance: 0.810 (must_read)

Oriented object detection in synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery often struggles to generalize to unknown domains, due to severe distribution shifts. Single-domain generalization (single-DG) aims to tackle this challenge by learning robust representations from only one source domain. However, existing single-DG methods typically fail to capture the essential features of rotating objects, as they neglect the unique physical properties introduced by the SAR imaging process. To address this, this paper proposes the scattering characteristic-driven cross-attention model (SCCAM), which introduces a heuristic scattering representation to approximate the potential physical structures of SAR objects. Specifically, a pair of homologous images is constructed by perturbing the extremely low- and high-frequency components of the input SAR image, ensuring that they share domain-invariant information. The heuristic attribute scattering center (ASC) features are then extracted to guide a bidirectional cross-attention module, enabling progressive interactions between homologous features. This design effectively mitigates feature distribution discrepancies, thereby facilitating the learning of domain-invariant representations. Experimental results on six single-DG benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed SCCAM significantly outperforms multiple classic and state-of-the-art DG models in oriented object detection across unknown SAR domains.

item.title: One-Step Diffusion and Flow Distillation through Implicit Generator Matching
item.dc:creator: Zemin Huang
Weijian Luo
Zhengyang Geng
Guojun Qi
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Despite strong performances on many generative tasks, diffusion and flow matching models require a large number of sampling steps to generate high-quality images. This has motivated the community to develop effective methods to distill pre-trained models into more efficient models. In this paper, we present Implicit Generator Matching (IGM), a systematic approach to distill both pre-trained diffusion/flow matching models into one-step generator models, while maintaining almost the same sample generation ability as the original model, as well as being data-free with no need for training images. The key challenge is that the traditional diffusion/flow-matching loss is intractable to distill a teacher diffusion/flow model with an explicitly defined field into a student generator, whose field is defined implicitly. The main breakthrough, our Implicit Gradient Theorem, provides an exact and efficient gradient to directly optimize the student by aligning this implicit field with the teacher's. IGM shows strong empirical performance for one-step generators, setting new standards. On CIFAR10, our diffusion-based SIM achieves an FID score of 2.06, while flow-based FGM sets a flow-model record with a 3.08 FID. Scaling to text-to-image models, SIM distillation of PixArt- \alpha \alpha yields a leading 6.42 aesthetic score, surpassing SDXL-TURBO (5.33), and FGM distillation of SD3 achieves a competitive 0.65 GenEval score against multi-step accelerators like Hyper-SD3 (0.63). Published: 2026-03-23T20:07:33+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.805 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Zemin Huang; Weijian Luo; Zhengyang Geng; Guojun Qi

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3676894

Relevance: 0.805 (must_read)

Despite strong performances on many generative tasks, diffusion and flow matching models require a large number of sampling steps to generate high-quality images. This has motivated the community to develop effective methods to distill pre-trained models into more efficient models. In this paper, we present Implicit Generator Matching (IGM), a systematic approach to distill both pre-trained diffusion/flow matching models into one-step generator models, while maintaining almost the same sample generation ability as the original model, as well as being data-free with no need for training images. The key challenge is that the traditional diffusion/flow-matching loss is intractable to distill a teacher diffusion/flow model with an explicitly defined field into a student generator, whose field is defined implicitly. The main breakthrough, our Implicit Gradient Theorem, provides an exact and efficient gradient to directly optimize the student by aligning this implicit field with the teacher's. IGM shows strong empirical performance for one-step generators, setting new standards. On CIFAR10, our diffusion-based SIM achieves an FID score of 2.06, while flow-based FGM sets a flow-model record with a 3.08 FID. Scaling to text-to-image models, SIM distillation of PixArt- \alpha \alpha yields a leading 6.42 aesthetic score, surpassing SDXL-TURBO (5.33), and FGM distillation of SD3 achieves a competitive 0.65 GenEval score against multi-step accelerators like Hyper-SD3 (0.63).

item.title: Interpretability and implicit model semantics in biomedicine and deep learning
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Warrell
Michael Gancz
Hussein Mohsen
Prashant Emani
Mark Gerstein
item.prism:publicationName: Nature Machine Intelligence
item.description: We introduce a framework to analyse interpretability in deep learning, by drawing on a formal notion of model semantics from the philosophy of science. We argue that interpretability is only one aspect of a model’s semantics and illustrate our framework with examples from biomedicine. Published: 2026-03-23T10:02:29+00:00 Venue: Nature Machine Intelligence Score: 0.805 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jonathan Warrell; Michael Gancz; Hussein Mohsen; Prashant Emani; Mark Gerstein

Journal: Nature Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1038/s42256-026-01177-0

Relevance: 0.805 (must_read)

We introduce a framework to analyse interpretability in deep learning, by drawing on a formal notion of model semantics from the philosophy of science. We argue that interpretability is only one aspect of a model’s semantics and illustrate our framework with examples from biomedicine.

item.title: GSFFE-Net: Global Semantic Feature Fusion Enhancement Network for Multi-Class Multi-Target Detection in SAR Images
item.dc:creator: Zekang Fan
Lingjun Zhao
Kui Liu
Kefeng Ji
Gangyao Kuang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
item.description: Multi-class multi-target detection in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery aims to simultaneously, rapidly, and efficiently detect and recognize multiple targets of interest within SAR images. It plays a crucial role in both military and civilian applications. However, most existing SAR target detection algorithms are designed for single target classes. When applied to multi-class detection scenarios, they often require multiple passes over the same scene using a series of single-class models, which results in poor performance, high computational costs, and long processing times. The development of algorithms capable of detecting multiple target classes simultaneously can effectively address these challenges. Nevertheless, compared to single-class detection methods, multi-class detection algorithms must account for more complex inter-class similarities and intra-class variations. Additionally, challenges such as multi scale variations caused by diverse target sizes, background clutter, and terrain interference become more pronounced in multi-class detection scenarios. In response to the above issues, this paper proposes a novel Global Semantic Feature Fusion Enhancement Network (GSFFE-Net) to address multi-class SAR target detection with enhanced global context modeling. The proposed framework integrates three key modules: 1) Wavelet Feature Recombination Module (WFRM), which decomposes SAR images into low-frequency background semantic features and high-frequency target details via 2D wavelet transform, then recombines multi-channel features to exploit prior spatial frequency information. 2) Global Semantic Attention Spatial Pyramid Pooling Fast (GSA-SPPF) module, which employs dual global pooling branches to capture global context, combined with a Multi-Scale Convolutional Attention (MSCA) mechanism to enhance cross-channel feature synergy. 3) CMUNeXt Block, inspired by medical image segmentation, which uses large-kernel depthwise separable convolutions and inver... Multi-class multi-target detection in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery aims to simultaneously, rapidly, and efficiently detect and recognize multiple targets of interest within SAR images. It plays a crucial role in both military and civilian applications. However, most existing SAR target detection algorithms are designed for single target classes. When applied to multi-class detection scenarios, they often require multiple passes over the same scene using a series of single-class models, which results in poor performance, high computational costs, and long processing times. Nevertheless, compared to single-class detection methods, multi-class detection algorithms must account for more complex inter-class similarities and intra-class variations. Additionally, challenges such as multi scale variations caused by diverse target sizes, background clutter, and terrain interference become more pronounced in multi-class detection scenarios. In response to the above issues, this paper proposes a novel Global Semantic Feature Fusion Enhancement Network (GSFFE-Net) to address multi-class SAR target detection with enhanced global context modeling. The proposed framework integrates three key modules: 1) Wavelet Feature Recombination Module (WFRM), which decomposes SAR images into low-frequency background semantic features and high-frequency target details via 2D wavelet transform, then recombines multi-channel features to exploit prior spatial frequency information. 2) Global Semantic Attention Spatial Pyramid Pooling Fast (GSA-SPPF) module, which employs dual global pooling branches to capture global context, combined with a Multi-Scale Convolutional Attention (MSCA) mechanism to enhance cross-channel feature synergy. 3) CMUNeXt Block, inspired by medical image segmentation, which uses large-kernel depthwise separable convolutions and inver...

item.title: Out-of-Sight Embodied Agents: Multimodal Tracking, Sensor Fusion, and Trajectory Forecasting
item.dc:creator: Haichao Zhang
Yi Xu
Yun Fu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
item.description: Trajectory prediction is a fundamental problem in computer vision, vision-language-action models, world models, and autonomous systems, with broad impact on applications including autonomous driving, robotics, and surveillance. Most existing approaches assume observations are complete and relatively clean, and thus do not adequately address out-ofsight agents or the intrinsic noise in sensing modalities (e.g., sensor measurements) caused by restricted camera coverage, occlusions, and the lack of ground-truth denoised trajectories. These factors introduce substantial safety concerns and reduce the robustness of trajectory prediction in practical deployments. In this extended study, we introduce major improvements to Out-of-Sight Trajectory (OST), a new task aimed at predicting noise-free visual trajectories of out-of-sight objects from noisy sensor observations. Based on our prior work, we expand the setting of Out-of-Sight Trajectory Prediction (OOSTraj) from pedestrians to both pedestrians and vehicles, thereby increasing its relevance to autonomous driving, robotics, and surveillance scenarios. Our improved Vision-Positioning Denoising Module utilizes camera calibration to construct a vision-position correspondence, mitigating the absence of direct visual cues while enabling effective unsupervised denoising of noisy sensor signals. Extensive experiments on the Vi-Fi and JRDB datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art results for both trajectory denoising and trajectory prediction, with clear gains over prior baselines. We further provide comparisons against classical denoising techniques, including Kalman filtering, and adapt recent trajectory prediction models to this setting, establishing a stronger and more comprehensive benchmark. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to incorporate vision-positioning projection to denoise noisy sensor trajectories of out-of-sight agents, opening new directions for future research in this are... Published: 2026-03-23T20:07:33+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence Score: 0.798 (must_read)

Authors: Haichao Zhang; Yi Xu; Yun Fu

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

DOI: 10.1109/tpami.2026.3676710

Relevance: 0.798 (must_read)

Trajectory prediction is a fundamental problem in computer vision, vision-language-action models, world models, and autonomous systems, with broad impact on applications including autonomous driving, robotics, and surveillance. Most existing approaches assume observations are complete and relatively clean, and thus do not adequately address out-ofsight agents or the intrinsic noise in sensing modalities (e.g., sensor measurements) caused by restricted camera coverage, occlusions, and the lack of ground-truth denoised trajectories. These factors introduce substantial safety concerns and reduce the robustness of trajectory prediction in practical deployments. In this extended study, we introduce major improvements to Out-of-Sight Trajectory (OST), a new task aimed at predicting noise-free visual trajectories of out-of-sight objects from noisy sensor observations. Based on our prior work, we expand the setting of Out-of-Sight Trajectory Prediction (OOSTraj) from pedestrians to both pedestrians and vehicles, thereby increasing its relevance to autonomous driving, robotics, and surveillance scenarios. Our improved Vision-Positioning Denoising Module utilizes camera calibration to construct a vision-position correspondence, mitigating the absence of direct visual cues while enabling effective unsupervised denoising of noisy sensor signals. Extensive experiments on the Vi-Fi and JRDB datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art results for both trajectory denoising and trajectory prediction, with clear gains over prior baselines. We further provide comparisons against classical denoising techniques, including Kalman filtering, and adapt recent trajectory prediction models to this setting, establishing a stronger and more comprehensive benchmark. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work to incorporate vision-positioning projection to denoise noisy sensor trajectories of out-of-sight agents, opening new directions for future research in this are...

item.title: BiKD: Bidomain Knowledge Distillation for SAR Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Xueyi Wang
Wenjia Xu
Guangzuo Li
Kuan Lin
Zhongqiu Xu
Yirong Wu
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
item.description: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery plays a pivotal role in remote sensing due to its all-weather and all-time imaging capabilities. Despite its advantages, deploying SAR object detection models on resource-limited platforms remains challenging. Existing knowledge distillation methods, primarily designed for natural images, fail to address the unique characteristics of SAR imagery, such as pronounced background clutter and pervasive speckle noise. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel bidomain knowledge distillation (BiKD) framework tailored for SAR object detection. BiKD integrates Frequency Alignment Distillation (FAD), which enhances discriminative structural representations by aligning high-frequency components between teacher and student detectors, and Spatial Feature Distillation (SFD), which improves core visual patterns learning by transferring channel-wise spatial attention distributions. Furthermore, a cross-head logit mimicking strategy is employed to alleviate target conflict during prediction supervision. Comprehensive experiments on the SSDD and SAR-AIRcraft-1.0 datasets demonstrate that BiKD consistently achieves superior performance, achieving significant improvements in detection accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency. Published: 2026-03-23T20:12:41+00:00 Venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters Score: 0.797 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xueyi Wang; Wenjia Xu; Guangzuo Li; Kuan Lin; Zhongqiu Xu; Yirong Wu

Journal: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters

DOI: 10.1109/lgrs.2026.3676449

Relevance: 0.797 (must_read)

Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery plays a pivotal role in remote sensing due to its all-weather and all-time imaging capabilities. Despite its advantages, deploying SAR object detection models on resource-limited platforms remains challenging. Existing knowledge distillation methods, primarily designed for natural images, fail to address the unique characteristics of SAR imagery, such as pronounced background clutter and pervasive speckle noise. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel bidomain knowledge distillation (BiKD) framework tailored for SAR object detection. BiKD integrates Frequency Alignment Distillation (FAD), which enhances discriminative structural representations by aligning high-frequency components between teacher and student detectors, and Spatial Feature Distillation (SFD), which improves core visual patterns learning by transferring channel-wise spatial attention distributions. Furthermore, a cross-head logit mimicking strategy is employed to alleviate target conflict during prediction supervision. Comprehensive experiments on the SSDD and SAR-AIRcraft-1.0 datasets demonstrate that BiKD consistently achieves superior performance, achieving significant improvements in detection accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.

item.title: UAV-DETR: DETR for Anti-Drone Target Detection
item.dc:creator: Jun Yang
Dong Wang
Hongxu Yin
Hongpeng Li
Jianxiong Yu
item.prism:publicationName: arXiv
item.description: Drone detection is pivotal in numerous security and counter-UAV applications. However, existing deep learning-based methods typically struggle to balance robust feature representation with computational efficiency. This challenge is particularly acute when detecting miniature drones against complex backgrounds under severe environmental interference. To address these issues, we introduce UAV-DETR, a novel framework that integrates a small-target-friendly architecture with real-time detection capabilities. Specifically, UAV-DETR features a WTConv-enhanced backbone and a Sliding Window Self-Attention (SWSA-IFI) encoder, capturing the high-frequency structural details of tiny targets while drastically reducing parameter overhead. Furthermore, we propose an Efficient Cross-Scale Feature Recalibration and Fusion Network (ECFRFN) to suppress background noise and aggregate multi-scale semantics. To further enhance accuracy, UAV-DETR incorporates a hybrid Inner-CIoU and NWD loss strategy, mitigating the extreme sensitivity of standard IoU metrics to minor positional deviations in small objects. Extensive experiments demonstrate that UAV-DETR significantly outperforms the baseline RT-DETR on our custom UAV dataset (+6.61% in mAP50:95, with a 39.8% reduction in parameters) and the public DUT-ANTI-UAV benchmark (+1.4% in Precision, +1.0% in F1-Score). These results establish UAV-DETR as a superior trade-off between efficiency and precision in counter-UAV object detection. The code is available at https://github.com/wd-sir/UAVDETR. Published: 2026-03-24T06:29:51+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.795 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Jun Yang; Dong Wang; Hongxu Yin; Hongpeng Li; Jianxiong Yu

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.795 (must_read)

Drone detection is pivotal in numerous security and counter-UAV applications. However, existing deep learning-based methods typically struggle to balance robust feature representation with computational efficiency. This challenge is particularly acute when detecting miniature drones against complex backgrounds under severe environmental interference. To address these issues, we introduce UAV-DETR, a novel framework that integrates a small-target-friendly architecture with real-time detection capabilities. Specifically, UAV-DETR features a WTConv-enhanced backbone and a Sliding Window Self-Attention (SWSA-IFI) encoder, capturing the high-frequency structural details of tiny targets while drastically reducing parameter overhead. Furthermore, we propose an Efficient Cross-Scale Feature Recalibration and Fusion Network (ECFRFN) to suppress background noise and aggregate multi-scale semantics. To further enhance accuracy, UAV-DETR incorporates a hybrid Inner-CIoU and NWD loss strategy, mitigating the extreme sensitivity of standard IoU metrics to minor positional deviations in small objects. Extensive experiments demonstrate that UAV-DETR significantly outperforms the baseline RT-DETR on our custom UAV dataset (+6.61% in mAP50:95, with a 39.8% reduction in parameters) and the public DUT-ANTI-UAV benchmark (+1.4% in Precision, +1.0% in F1-Score). These results establish UAV-DETR as a superior trade-off between efficiency and precision in counter-UAV object detection. The code is available at https://github.com/wd-sir/UAVDETR.

item.title: CFSPNet: Cross-domain Feature Synergy and Perception for Robust Underwater Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Dechuan Kong
Yandi Zhang
Xiaohu Zhao
Wenyi Zhao
Deguang Li
Weidong Zhang
item.prism:publicationName: Expert Systems with Applications
item.description: Underwater object detection is essential for marine resource exploration, ecological monitoring, and autonomous underwater vehicle navigation. However, the challenges of underwater environments, including light absorption, scattering, and color distortion, severely degrade the quality and reliability of visual information, affecting the effective execution of underwater tasks. We propose the Cross-domain Feature Synergy and Perception framework, CFSPNet, to confront these issues. Specifically, we first introduce the Target-Aware Detection Enhancement Network, TADENet, to restore degraded underwater images, generating enhanced domain representations with latent structural and semantic details. Subsequently, Cross-domain Prior-guided Feature Synergy integrates features from the source and enhanced domains to jointly capture multi-domain semantic and structural depth information. Building upon this, the Multi-scale Wavelet Cascade Perception module further extracts multi-scale wavelet-domain features to expand the network’s receptive field and enhance sensitivity to critical feature cues. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CFSPNet achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy and robustness, highlighting its potential for practical real-world underwater applications. Published: 2026-03-24T23:50:03+00:00 Venue: Expert Systems with Applications Score: 0.795 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Dechuan Kong; Yandi Zhang; Xiaohu Zhao; Wenyi Zhao; Deguang Li; Weidong Zhang

Journal: Expert Systems with Applications

DOI: 10.1016/j.eswa.2026.132174

Relevance: 0.795 (must_read)

Underwater object detection is essential for marine resource exploration, ecological monitoring, and autonomous underwater vehicle navigation. However, the challenges of underwater environments, including light absorption, scattering, and color distortion, severely degrade the quality and reliability of visual information, affecting the effective execution of underwater tasks. We propose the Cross-domain Feature Synergy and Perception framework, CFSPNet, to confront these issues. Specifically, we first introduce the Target-Aware Detection Enhancement Network, TADENet, to restore degraded underwater images, generating enhanced domain representations with latent structural and semantic details. Subsequently, Cross-domain Prior-guided Feature Synergy integrates features from the source and enhanced domains to jointly capture multi-domain semantic and structural depth information. Building upon this, the Multi-scale Wavelet Cascade Perception module further extracts multi-scale wavelet-domain features to expand the network’s receptive field and enhance sensitivity to critical feature cues. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CFSPNet achieves state-of-the-art detection accuracy and robustness, highlighting its potential for practical real-world underwater applications.

item.title: Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing Image Semantic Segmentation with Prototype-Driven Domain Disentangle Alignment
item.dc:creator: Xiufei Zhang
Yuanwei Liu
Xiaoliang Qian
Gong Cheng
Xiwen Yao
item.prism:publicationName: Pattern Recognition
item.description: Existing remote sensing domain adaptation (DA) methods for semantic segmentation typically achieve domain feature alignment by learning domain-invariant features. However, these methods may inadvertently retain domain-specific features within the extracted representations, resulting in insufficient alignment of domain semantic feature information. To address this issue, we propose a prototype-driven domain disentangled alignment (PDDA) framework to mitigate the impact of domain-specific information from extracted features on the misalignment of semantic feature information. Specifically, we design a domain feature disentangle with prototypes (DFDP) method to automatically disentangle domain-invariant and domain-specific representations by explicitly modeling domain-invariant and domain-specific prototypes, respectively. Then, we construct a domain-specific guided feature alignment (DSFA) strategy, which leverages domain-specific representations as complementary guidance to improve the semantic alignment of domain-invariant features. In addition, we propose a domain-invariant induced self-learning (DISL) mechanism, which exploits pseudo-label learning guided by domain-invariant features to further strengthen cross-domain semantic feature alignment. Extensive experiments on both ISPRS (Potsdam and Vaihingen) and LoveDA (Rural and Urban) datasets robustly attest to the preeminence of the proposed PDDA over other advanced DA methods. The code will be available at https://github.com/XZhang878/PDDA . Published: 2026-03-24T00:09:27+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.794 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Xiufei Zhang; Yuanwei Liu; Xiaoliang Qian; Gong Cheng; Xiwen Yao

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113583

Relevance: 0.794 (must_read)

Existing remote sensing domain adaptation (DA) methods for semantic segmentation typically achieve domain feature alignment by learning domain-invariant features. However, these methods may inadvertently retain domain-specific features within the extracted representations, resulting in insufficient alignment of domain semantic feature information. To address this issue, we propose a prototype-driven domain disentangled alignment (PDDA) framework to mitigate the impact of domain-specific information from extracted features on the misalignment of semantic feature information. Specifically, we design a domain feature disentangle with prototypes (DFDP) method to automatically disentangle domain-invariant and domain-specific representations by explicitly modeling domain-invariant and domain-specific prototypes, respectively. Then, we construct a domain-specific guided feature alignment (DSFA) strategy, which leverages domain-specific representations as complementary guidance to improve the semantic alignment of domain-invariant features. In addition, we propose a domain-invariant induced self-learning (DISL) mechanism, which exploits pseudo-label learning guided by domain-invariant features to further strengthen cross-domain semantic feature alignment. Extensive experiments on both ISPRS (Potsdam and Vaihingen) and LoveDA (Rural and Urban) datasets robustly attest to the preeminence of the proposed PDDA over other advanced DA methods. The code will be available at https://github.com/XZhang878/PDDA .

item.title: Clarity-Former: Tackling Image Degradation and Geometric Diversity in Underwater Instance Segmentation
item.dc:creator: Houru Chen
Li Feng
Qinglin Zhao
Yi Sun
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Effective visual analysis of underwater environments for applications in geoscience and remote sensing is fundamentally constrained by two principal factors: severe image degradation inherent to the aquatic medium and the extreme geometric diversity of instances. Existing methods often struggle as they rely on separate enhancement modules or complex architectures that are not optimized for these conditions. To overcome these limitations, we propose Clarity-Former, a novel one-stage, end-to-end Transformer framework designed with a synergistic architecture to overcome both challenges simultaneously. The core of our framework is a Self-Adaptive Masked Attention (SAMA) transformer backbone, which uses an adaptive temperature and a diagonal masking strategy to extract robust local features directly from noisy, low-contrast inputs. Building on this robust feature foundation, a Transformer Encoder-Decoder module with a sparse attention sampling mechanism efficiently models long-range, multi-scale dependencies to handle vast variations in instance scale and shape. Finally, a Variance-Guided Spatial Enhancement (VGSE) module intelligently fuses and refines multi-scale features to ensure the generation of high-quality segmentation masks. Extensive experiments on the UIIS, USIS10K, and USIS16 datasets demonstrate that Clarity-Former achieves an optimal trade-off between efficiency and precision. Specifically, our Small variant surpasses the previous best method WaterMask by 2.8%, 5.8%, and 2.5% mAP, respectively. Furthermore, even with the minimal parameter (45M), our Tiny variant consistently outperforms existing baselines on all three datasets. This positions Clarity-Former as a highly efficient framework for automated underwater visual analysis. Published: 2026-03-23T20:07:27+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.793 (must_read)

Authors: Houru Chen; Li Feng; Qinglin Zhao; Yi Sun

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3676605

Relevance: 0.793 (must_read)

Effective visual analysis of underwater environments for applications in geoscience and remote sensing is fundamentally constrained by two principal factors: severe image degradation inherent to the aquatic medium and the extreme geometric diversity of instances. Existing methods often struggle as they rely on separate enhancement modules or complex architectures that are not optimized for these conditions. To overcome these limitations, we propose Clarity-Former, a novel one-stage, end-to-end Transformer framework designed with a synergistic architecture to overcome both challenges simultaneously. The core of our framework is a Self-Adaptive Masked Attention (SAMA) transformer backbone, which uses an adaptive temperature and a diagonal masking strategy to extract robust local features directly from noisy, low-contrast inputs. Building on this robust feature foundation, a Transformer Encoder-Decoder module with a sparse attention sampling mechanism efficiently models long-range, multi-scale dependencies to handle vast variations in instance scale and shape. Finally, a Variance-Guided Spatial Enhancement (VGSE) module intelligently fuses and refines multi-scale features to ensure the generation of high-quality segmentation masks. Extensive experiments on the UIIS, USIS10K, and USIS16 datasets demonstrate that Clarity-Former achieves an optimal trade-off between efficiency and precision. Specifically, our Small variant surpasses the previous best method WaterMask by 2.8%, 5.8%, and 2.5% mAP, respectively. Furthermore, even with the minimal parameter (45M), our Tiny variant consistently outperforms existing baselines on all three datasets. This positions Clarity-Former as a highly efficient framework for automated underwater visual analysis.

item.title: “Stones from Other Hills can Polish Jade”: Zero-shot Anomaly Synthesis via Cross-domain Anomaly Injection
item.dc:creator: Siqi Wang
Yuanze Hu
Xinwang Liu
Siwei Wang
Guangpu Wang
Chuanfu Xu
Jie Liu
Ping Chen
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Industrial image anomaly detection (IAD) is a pivotal topic with huge value. Due to the nature of anomalies, real anomalies in a specific modern industrial domain (i.e., domain-specific anomalies) are usually too rare to collect, which severely hinders IAD. Thus, zero-shot anomaly synthesis (ZSAS), which synthesizes pseudo anomaly images without any domain-specific anomaly, emerges as a vital technique for IAD. However, existing solutions are either unable to synthesize authentic pseudo anomalies, or require cumbersome training. Thus, we focus on ZSAS and propose a brand-new paradigm that can realize both authentic and training-free ZSAS. It is based on a chronically-ignored fact: Although domain-specific anomalies are rare, real anomalies from other domains (i.e., cross-domain anomalies) are actually abundant and directly applicable to ZSAS. Specifically, our new ZSAS paradigm makes three-fold contributions: First, we propose a novel method named Cross-domain Anomaly Injection (CAI), which directly exploits cross-domain anomalies to enable highly authentic ZSAS in a training-free manner. Second, to supply CAI with sufficient cross-domain anomalies, we build the first Domain-agnostic Anomaly Dataset (DAAD) within our best knowledge, which provides ZSAS with abundant real anomaly patterns. Third, we propose a CAI-guided Diffusion Mechanism, which can further break the quantity limit of real anomalies and enable unlimited anomaly synthesis. Our head-to-head comparison with existing ZSAS solutions justifies the superior performance of our paradigm for IAD and demonstrates it as an effective and pragmatic ZSAS solution. Published: 2026-03-24T19:50:48+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.792 (must_read)

Authors: Siqi Wang; Yuanze Hu; Xinwang Liu; Siwei Wang; Guangpu Wang; Chuanfu Xu; Jie Liu; Ping Chen

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3675432

Relevance: 0.792 (must_read)

Industrial image anomaly detection (IAD) is a pivotal topic with huge value. Due to the nature of anomalies, real anomalies in a specific modern industrial domain (i.e., domain-specific anomalies) are usually too rare to collect, which severely hinders IAD. Thus, zero-shot anomaly synthesis (ZSAS), which synthesizes pseudo anomaly images without any domain-specific anomaly, emerges as a vital technique for IAD. However, existing solutions are either unable to synthesize authentic pseudo anomalies, or require cumbersome training. Thus, we focus on ZSAS and propose a brand-new paradigm that can realize both authentic and training-free ZSAS. It is based on a chronically-ignored fact: Although domain-specific anomalies are rare, real anomalies from other domains (i.e., cross-domain anomalies) are actually abundant and directly applicable to ZSAS. Specifically, our new ZSAS paradigm makes three-fold contributions: First, we propose a novel method named Cross-domain Anomaly Injection (CAI), which directly exploits cross-domain anomalies to enable highly authentic ZSAS in a training-free manner. Second, to supply CAI with sufficient cross-domain anomalies, we build the first Domain-agnostic Anomaly Dataset (DAAD) within our best knowledge, which provides ZSAS with abundant real anomaly patterns. Third, we propose a CAI-guided Diffusion Mechanism, which can further break the quantity limit of real anomalies and enable unlimited anomaly synthesis. Our head-to-head comparison with existing ZSAS solutions justifies the superior performance of our paradigm for IAD and demonstrates it as an effective and pragmatic ZSAS solution.

item.title: DepMatch: Boosting Semi-supervised Semantic Segmentation by Exploring Depth Difference Knowledge
item.dc:creator: Jianjian Yin
Xiruo Jiang
Tao Chen
Gensheng Pei
Yazhou Yao
Fumin Shen
Heng-Tao Shen
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
item.description: Existing semi-supervised semantic segmentation (SSS) methods fail to explore the potential of depth information in unlabeled data, as they suffer from i) inter-class depth similarity, and ii) intra-class depth discrepancy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes DepMatch, a simple yet effective approach that leverages depth difference knowledge to guide consistency learning. Specifically, a Class-wise Depth Disparity Perception (CDDP) module is designed to exploit depth difference information, driven by class prediction priors, facilitating robust feature learning. Depth-feature discrepancy set is first constructed and then reliable pixel pairs are selected for inter-class depth disparity knowledge distillation. Simultaneously, exponential normalization is applied to intra-category depth disparity for suppressing large outlier variations, and an entropy-based adaptive weight is derived to prioritize feature learning of high entropy areas. Moreover, we propose the Uncertain Logit Disparity Regulation (ULDR) module, which leverages the depth variations at class boundaries to promote the mutual regulation of uncertain pixel logit information, enhancing the model’s spatial understanding. Experiments on five public benchmarks show that DepMatch can be seamlessly incorporated as a plug-and-play plugin into popular SSS frameworks, achieving significant performance improvements across various visual encoders. The source code and models are made available at https://github.com/NUST-Machine-Intelligence-Laboratory/DepMatch. Published: 2026-03-24T19:50:48+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing Score: 0.788 (must_read)

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing

DOI: 10.1109/tip.2026.3675487

Relevance: 0.788 (must_read)

Existing semi-supervised semantic segmentation (SSS) methods fail to explore the potential of depth information in unlabeled data, as they suffer from i) inter-class depth similarity, and ii) intra-class depth discrepancy. To address these challenges, this paper proposes DepMatch, a simple yet effective approach that leverages depth difference knowledge to guide consistency learning. Specifically, a Class-wise Depth Disparity Perception (CDDP) module is designed to exploit depth difference information, driven by class prediction priors, facilitating robust feature learning. Depth-feature discrepancy set is first constructed and then reliable pixel pairs are selected for inter-class depth disparity knowledge distillation. Simultaneously, exponential normalization is applied to intra-category depth disparity for suppressing large outlier variations, and an entropy-based adaptive weight is derived to prioritize feature learning of high entropy areas. Moreover, we propose the Uncertain Logit Disparity Regulation (ULDR) module, which leverages the depth variations at class boundaries to promote the mutual regulation of uncertain pixel logit information, enhancing the model’s spatial understanding. Experiments on five public benchmarks show that DepMatch can be seamlessly incorporated as a plug-and-play plugin into popular SSS frameworks, achieving significant performance improvements across various visual encoders. The source code and models are made available at https://github.com/NUST-Machine-Intelligence-Laboratory/DepMatch.

item.title: Deep learning in medical image analysis for bone tumor: A comprehensive survey
item.dc:creator: Mingyang Jiang
Shanshan Cai
Ke Zhang
Meng Li
Yu Tian
Tao Wang
Raquel Alarcón Rodríguez
Zheng Wang
Sichang Wu
Jiaqi He
Zihao Yuan
Liuru Lu
Guichuan Lai
Chengcheng Zhang
Zhandong Bo
Ruqiong Wei
item.prism:publicationName: Information Fusion
item.description: The heterogeneity and rarity of Bone Tumors (BTs) make early and accurate diagnosis challenging. Although Deep Learning (DL) has achieved remarkable success in medical image analysis, its applications in BT imaging remain fragmented and lack systematic synthesis. This survey aims to systematically summarize and critically analyze DL-based methods for BT medical image analysis, covering both classification and lesion localization tasks, while identifying key technical trends, challenges, and future directions. Following established systematic review guidelines, we searched five major databases (e.g., PubMed, Elsevier) for studies published between 2018 and 2025. Based on predefined criteria, 46 eligible studies were selected. These studies were then systematically analyzed using a proposed methodological taxonomy. Three representative public datasets and their characteristics are summarized. DL methods are categorized into classification (CNN-based, ViT-based, CNN-ViT hybrid architectures, and label-efficient learning) and localization (pixel-level segmentation and object detection). Comparative analysis shows that hybrid architectures and label-efficient strategies partially alleviate data scarcity, while U-Net- and YOLO-based frameworks remain the dominant choices for segmentation and detection, respectively. However, most studies suffer from limited dataset scale, insufficient interpretability, and weak cross-institutional generalization. This survey provides a comprehensive and critical synthesis of DL applications in multimodal BT imaging. Future progress will depend on high-quality multicenter datasets, interpretable and robust algorithms, and rigorous prospective clinical validation, thereby supporting the translation of DL-assisted bone tumor diagnosis into clinical practice. Published: 2026-03-23T07:17:28+00:00 Venue: Information Fusion Score: 0.786 (must_read)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mingyang Jiang; Shanshan Cai; Ke Zhang; Meng Li; Yu Tian; Tao Wang; Raquel Alarcón Rodríguez; Zheng Wang; Sichang Wu; Jiaqi He; Zihao Yuan; Liuru Lu; Guichuan Lai; Chengcheng Zhang; Zhandong Bo; Ruqiong Wei

Journal: Information Fusion

DOI: 10.1016/j.inffus.2026.104305

Relevance: 0.786 (must_read)

The heterogeneity and rarity of Bone Tumors (BTs) make early and accurate diagnosis challenging. Although Deep Learning (DL) has achieved remarkable success in medical image analysis, its applications in BT imaging remain fragmented and lack systematic synthesis. This survey aims to systematically summarize and critically analyze DL-based methods for BT medical image analysis, covering both classification and lesion localization tasks, while identifying key technical trends, challenges, and future directions. Following established systematic review guidelines, we searched five major databases (e.g., PubMed, Elsevier) for studies published between 2018 and 2025. Based on predefined criteria, 46 eligible studies were selected. These studies were then systematically analyzed using a proposed methodological taxonomy. Three representative public datasets and their characteristics are summarized. DL methods are categorized into classification (CNN-based, ViT-based, CNN-ViT hybrid architectures, and label-efficient learning) and localization (pixel-level segmentation and object detection). Comparative analysis shows that hybrid architectures and label-efficient strategies partially alleviate data scarcity, while U-Net- and YOLO-based frameworks remain the dominant choices for segmentation and detection, respectively. However, most studies suffer from limited dataset scale, insufficient interpretability, and weak cross-institutional generalization. This survey provides a comprehensive and critical synthesis of DL applications in multimodal BT imaging. Future progress will depend on high-quality multicenter datasets, interpretable and robust algorithms, and rigorous prospective clinical validation, thereby supporting the translation of DL-assisted bone tumor diagnosis into clinical practice.

item.title: TAWNet: Three-dimensional Adaptive Weighted Network for RGB-D Salient Object Detection
item.dc:creator: Jiazheng Wu
Zhenxue Chen
Qingqiang Guo
Chengyun Liu
Zhenyan Wang
Qinggang Meng
item.prism:publicationName: Knowledge-Based Systems
item.description: Although recent RGB-D salient object detection methods have explored various cross-modal fusion strategies, ranging from modality-specific processing to different operations targeting high- and low-level features, they typically lack a systematic framework for jointly modeling the intrinsic relationships between modalities. This limits their ability to exploit the synergistic potential of RGB and depth modalities fully. Furthermore, the quality of the input depth maps and the cross-scale interaction between multi-level features are also concerns. To address these issues, we propose a Three-dimensional Adaptive Weighted Network (TAWNet), which explicitly and collaboratively models the similarity, difference, and complementarity between modalities to achieve effective cross-modal fusion. Specifically, TAWNet employs a P2T-based parallel encoder to extract hierarchical features. The lightweight Depth Quality Optimization module (DQO) is first applied to the depth branch for filtering and enhancing depth features. The core of our framework is the Three-dimensional Adaptive Weighted Fusion module (TAWF), which first applies different purification processes to the two modal inputs. Then, it learns adaptive weights across three dimensions: semantic similarity, modal difference, and information complementarity, to perform weighted fusion and generate unified fused features, seamlessly integrating the purified multimodal information. Furthermore, the Cross-scale Residual Attention module (CRA) in the decoder progressively integrates multi-scale contextual information through a cascaded structure for refined prediction. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that, through the synergistic effect of the three core modules, our TAWNet outperforms 21 state-of-the-art methods. More importantly, TAWNet achieves a superior balance between performance and complexity, providing excellent accuracy without introducing excessive parameters and computational costs. The source codes will be released at https://github.com/Karry-Wu/TAWNet . Published: 2026-03-24T07:53:25+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.785 (must_read)

Authors: Jiazheng Wu; Zhenxue Chen; Qingqiang Guo; Chengyun Liu; Zhenyan Wang; Qinggang Meng

Journal: Knowledge-Based Systems

DOI: 10.1016/j.knosys.2026.115854

Relevance: 0.785 (must_read)

Although recent RGB-D salient object detection methods have explored various cross-modal fusion strategies, ranging from modality-specific processing to different operations targeting high- and low-level features, they typically lack a systematic framework for jointly modeling the intrinsic relationships between modalities. This limits their ability to exploit the synergistic potential of RGB and depth modalities fully. Furthermore, the quality of the input depth maps and the cross-scale interaction between multi-level features are also concerns. To address these issues, we propose a Three-dimensional Adaptive Weighted Network (TAWNet), which explicitly and collaboratively models the similarity, difference, and complementarity between modalities to achieve effective cross-modal fusion. Specifically, TAWNet employs a P2T-based parallel encoder to extract hierarchical features. The lightweight Depth Quality Optimization module (DQO) is first applied to the depth branch for filtering and enhancing depth features. The core of our framework is the Three-dimensional Adaptive Weighted Fusion module (TAWF), which first applies different purification processes to the two modal inputs. Then, it learns adaptive weights across three dimensions: semantic similarity, modal difference, and information complementarity, to perform weighted fusion and generate unified fused features, seamlessly integrating the purified multimodal information. Furthermore, the Cross-scale Residual Attention module (CRA) in the decoder progressively integrates multi-scale contextual information through a cascaded structure for refined prediction. Extensive experiments on six benchmark datasets demonstrate that, through the synergistic effect of the three core modules, our TAWNet outperforms 21 state-of-the-art methods. More importantly, TAWNet achieves a superior balance between performance and complexity, providing excellent accuracy without introducing excessive parameters and computational costs. The source codes will be released at https://github.com/Karry-Wu/TAWNet .

item.title: Towards robust disparity estimation in satellite stereo imagery: a new high-quality benchmark dataset and a metadata-informed multi-range geometric encoding network

item.description: In satellite remote sensing, disparity estimation is a core technology for three-dimensional reconstruction. However, satellite stereo image disparity estimation faces two primary challenges: 1) The limitations in the quality and scale of available datasets. 2) The persistent difficulty for current methods to simultaneously achieve both fine-grained small-scale and robust large-scale disparity estimation. To address these issues, this study builds a satellite stereo image disparity estimation dataset (WHU-SSIDE) using GaoFen-7 stereo images and open-source LiDAR data through a meticulously designed technical pipeline. The dataset comprises 3,737 data pairs, each containing left-view and right-view images, high-quality disparity ground truth, and corresponding metadata. The dataset construction pipeline incorporates a multi-level geometry correction mechanism that ensures sub-pixel accuracy (MAE https://github.com/zhanggb1997/WHU-SSIDE .
